title: Tbx1 Heterozygosity in the Oligodendrocyte Lineage Shifts Myelinated Axon Composition in the Mouse Fimbria Without Behavioral Impairments
abstract: Constitutive Tbx1 heterozygosity (model of 22q11.2 deletion syndrome, a high-penetrance NDD/psychosis risk CNV) produces behavioral deficits and altered myelinated axon composition. This paper dissects oligodendrocyte-lineage-specific Tbx1 heterozygosity and finds altered fimbria myelin composition WITHOUT behavioral deficits -- dissociating cell-lineage-specific myelin changes from behavioral phenotype.
methods: Conditional heterozygous mouse model, myelin composition analysis, behavioral phenotyping
summary: 22q11.2 / Tbx1 heterozygosity restricted to oligodendrocyte lineage shifts myelin composition but does not reproduce behavioral deficits, dissociating a cellular phenotype from behavior. Relevant to severity-disconnect workstream.
